title: Gastrochilus jerdonianus
taxonomy: Gastrochilus jerdonianus is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas. It is classified under the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Within this family, it belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Gastrochilinae. The genus Gastrochilus is a group of orchids characterized by their unique leaf arrangement and inflorescence structure. This species is a member of the Gastrochilus genus, which comprises approximately 60 species of orchids.

intro: Gastrochilus jerdonianus, also known as the Indian stomach orchid, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the Eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, diabetes, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Gastrochilus jerdonianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Gastrochilus jerdonianus is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions.
